title: Theme Agent Board webview with VS Code theme variables
brief: Board should follow the theme of vs code
description: The Agent Board webview currently hardcodes a dark Linear-style palette in webview/styles.css (:root defines --bg: #08090a, --panel, --text, --accent, etc., plus an Inter font stack). This makes the board ignore the user's active VS Code color theme: it stays dark on light themes, clashes with custom themes, and ignores high-contrast modes. The task is to restyle the board so it follows the active VS Code theme by mapping the existing CSS custom properties onto the --vscode-* CSS variables that VS Code injects into every webview (e.g. --vscode-editor-background, --vscode-sideBar-background, --vscode-editorWidget-background, --vscode-foreground, --vscode-descriptionForeground, --vscode-panel-border / --vscode-widget-border, --vscode-focusBorder, --vscode-button-background / --vscode-button-foreground / --vscode-button-hoverBackground, --vscode-input-background / --vscode-input-border, --vscode-list-hoverBackground, --vscode-badge-background, and status colors like --vscode-charts-green / --vscode-charts-orange / --vscode-errorForeground or the testing/notification colors). Typography should use var(--vscode-font-family), var(--vscode-font-size), and var(--vscode-editor-font-family) for monospace areas instead of the hardcoded Inter stack. The recommended approach is to keep the existing internal token names (--bg, --panel, --text, --muted, --accent, ...) and redefine each one in :root as var(--vscode-..., <current value>) with the current hex as fallback, so the ~110 existing var() usages across styles.css keep working and the diff stays small. Any remaining hardcoded hex/rgba values in styles.css (and any inline colors in webview/main.tsx) should be converted to those tokens or to color-mix()/opacity derived from theme variables. The board must remain legible in dark, light, and high-contrast themes and update live when the user switches themes (webviews get new variable values automatically; no reload logic should be required). Semantic status colors (green = done/success, amber = in progress/warning, red = blocked/error, accent = selection/primary actions) must stay distinguishable in both light and dark themes. No behavior, layout, or extension-host (src/) changes are expected beyond styling; the CSP in src/extension.ts already permits the bundled stylesheet.
acceptanceCriteria: webview/styles.css no longer hardcodes the page palette: every :root token resolves from a --vscode-* variable, with the previous hex value only as a fallback for non-webview contexts
Body/background, panels, cards, borders, and text colors visibly change to match the active VS Code theme when switching between a dark theme (e.g. Dark+) and a light theme (e.g. Light+)
Typography uses var(--vscode-font-family) and var(--vscode-font-size) for UI text instead of the hardcoded Inter stack; monospace areas (if any) use var(--vscode-editor-font-family)
Buttons, inputs, textareas, and selects use VS Code button/input theme variables (background, foreground, border, hover) and remain readable in light themes
Focus states use var(--vscode-focusBorder); hover states on list/board items use list hover variables rather than fixed white-alpha overlays
Status/priority colors (done, in-progress, blocked, priority badges) derive from theme variables or theme-safe values and remain distinguishable on both light and dark backgrounds
No hardcoded hex or rgba colors remain in webview/main.tsx inline styles (if any exist) — they are moved to CSS tokens
Theme switching while the board is open updates the UI live without reloading the webview or any code changes in src/extension.ts
High-contrast themes produce visible borders and readable text (no invisible controls due to transparent backgrounds)
Board layout, spacing, and behavior are unchanged — this is a color/typography retheme only
qaChecklist: Run npm run typecheck and npm run build; both pass with no new errors
Run npm run test (and npm run test:prd) to confirm no regressions in existing suites
Launch the extension (F5 / Extension Development Host), open the Agent Board panel, and verify it renders with the current theme's colors
Switch VS Code to Light+ (Preferences: Color Theme) with the board open — background turns light, all text remains readable, no white-on-white or dark-on-dark elements
Switch to Dark+ — board looks equivalent to (or better than) the previous dark design
Switch to a High Contrast theme — buttons, inputs, and card borders are visible and text meets contrast
Exercise key surfaces in each theme: setup screen, board columns, task cards, task detail view, buttons/menus, inputs and textareas, empty states
Verify status colors (done/in-progress/blocked, priority badges) are distinguishable in both light and dark themes
grep webview/styles.css for remaining raw hex values outside of var(--vscode-..., fallback) fallbacks and confirm each remaining one is intentional
Confirm the sidebar webview view and the panel webview (both created in src/extension.ts) render themed correctly
designQaChecklist: Board looks native to VS Code — surfaces align with editor/sideBar/editorWidget backgrounds rather than a custom black palette
Visual hierarchy preserved: panels vs cards vs page background remain distinguishable in light, dark, and high-contrast themes
Text contrast: primary text, muted text, and subtle text all remain legible in Light+ (muted text must not be too faint on white)
Accent/selection color follows the theme (focusBorder/button background) rather than the fixed indigo #5e6ad2, or degrades gracefully to it
Hover and focus states are perceivable in all themes; focus rings use --vscode-focusBorder
Font matches the VS Code UI font; sizes and spacing unchanged from the current design
Badges, chips, and status dots keep sufficient contrast against their backgrounds in both light and dark themes
No harsh pure-white or pure-black artifacts introduced when switching themes; shadows/overlays adapt (avoid fixed white-alpha lines on light backgrounds)
constraints: Keep the existing internal CSS custom-property names (--bg, --panel, --text, --accent, ...) and remap them to --vscode-* variables with fallbacks; avoid a wholesale rewrite of the ~990-line stylesheet
Styling-only change: do not alter board behavior, message passing, storage, or the webview HTML/CSP in src/extension.ts unless strictly required
Do not add new dependencies or a theming library; use plain CSS variables provided by the VS Code webview environment

Do not implement custom theme detection/toggle logic in JS — rely on VS Code's injected CSS variables and body theme classes (vscode-light / vscode-dark / vscode-high-contrast) if per-theme overrides are needed
Preserve current layout, spacing, radii, and typography scale; only colors and font-family sources change
Follow existing project patterns before introducing new abstractions, and keep changes scoped to this task
agentNotes: Remapped the existing CSS token system to VS Code webview variables, retaining the former palette only as non-webview fallbacks. Added theme tokens for primary/secondary buttons, inputs, links, semantic status colors, shadows, typography, and high-contrast borders. Removed fixed button/danger/shadow colors and the fixed-color select SVG; native select rendering now follows the host theme. Added focus-visible outlines using focusBorder. No layout, React, extension-host, dependency, or behavior changes were needed. Static color audit found raw colors only inside documented :root fallbacks.

qaNotes.message: Verified stylesheet-only scope, VS Code theme token coverage, high-contrast border precedence, themed controls/focus states, and raw colors restricted to documented fallbacks. Automated validation passes; interactive theme switching remains a Human Review visual check.
